rationale: Best trustless-decision use in the field: whether a PR actually satisfies an issue is subjective, valuable, and painful to arbitrate manually. GenLayer is doing real decision work here. Strong hackathon fit.
Freelance escrow and work acceptance is exactly the kind of gray-zone decision GenLayer should handle. Clear economic value, strong trustless-decision fit, and credible product direction.
Compliance and audit verification is a genuinely interesting trustless-decision category. It moves beyond toy demos and uses GenLayer for contested judgment, not just automation theater.
Very clean GenLayer fit: prediction markets need trustworthy resolution. Strong implementation fit, though the category itself is a familiar GenLayer use case rather than a novel one.
Good application of validator consensus to shipment/payment release. Trustless decision-making clearly matters because delivery quality and acceptance are hard to encode in ordinary smart contracts.

DAO treasury policy and constitutional compliance are high-value gray-zone decisions. Strong conceptual GenLayer fit, though product complexity makes the hackathon execution a bit harder to judge cleanly.
Very good GenLayer category: work submission acceptance and escrow release. Slightly less differentiated than VeriTask, but still a serious use of trustless AI judgment.
Interesting use of GenLayer for campaign validation, especially where fraud and legitimacy are fuzzy. Less obviously massive than escrow or bounties, but still a meaningful trustless-decision use.
Merchant/payment approval with AI validation is directionally strong. GenLayer is being used for decision-gating, though the product story is a bit more specialized.
AI judging is a plausible GenLayer use, especially for hackathon/build evaluation. Interesting, but less economically sharp than bounty, escrow, compliance, or treasury decisions.
